MCKESSON CORPORATION JANSSEN RESERACH & DEVELOPMENT, LLC FKA AND INCORRECLTY NAMED AS JOHNSON & JOHNSON PHARAMACEUTICAL RESEARCH & DEVELOPMENT, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED AS TO ALL ISSUES EXCEPT THE ISSUE OF "FEDERAL PREEMPTION." A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS MAY BE GRANTED ONLY IF THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE CHALLENGED PLEADING OR SUBJECT TO JUDICIAL NOTICE SHOW THAT THE PLEADING FAILS TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION. (COUNTY OF ORANGE V. ASSOCIATION OF ORANGE COUNTY DEPUTY SHERIFFS (2011) 192 CAL.APP.4TH 21, 32.) MCKESSON'S RELIANCE ON ANSWERS FILED BY DEFENDANTS IS THUS IMPERMISSIBLE. THE GENERAL RULE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW IS THAT DISTRIBUTORS OF DEFECTIVE PRODUCTS ARE STRICTLY LIABLE. PLAINTIFF'S FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS SUFFICIENTLY INVOKE THAT GENERAL RULE AND THUS SUPPORT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ASSERTED AGAINST MCKESSON. (BOSTICK V. FLEX EQUIPMENT CO. INC. (2007) 147 CAL.APP.4TH 80, 88; COMPLAINT. 22, 24, 32, 33, 43- 45, 66, 76-81, 84, 85, 88, 89, 91-95, 97-99, 101, 103, 104, 107-109, 111, 112, 115, 117, 118, 120, 123, 125-129, 130-134, 136, 138, 140, 141.) MCKESSON'S ARGUMENT IN RELIANCE ON BAY SUMMIT COMMUNITY ASSN. V. SHELL OIL CO. (1996) 51 CAL.APP.4TH 762 IS MISPLACED BECAUSE MCKESSON IS ALLEGED TO BE A DISTRIBUTOR WITHIN THE VERTICAL CHAIN OF DISTRIBUTION. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, AS TO THE "FEDERAL PREEMPTION" ISSUE, IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 8, 2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. SUPPLEMENTAL MOVING PAPERS ARE DUE BY 8/12/16; SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION BY 8/26/216; FIVE-PAGE LIMIT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. TEL: 530-339-5023. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Continued To Nov-23-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Mckesson Corporation'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings, As To The "Federal Preemption" Issue
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Kristen Barton and Ahmed S. Diab of Gomez Trial Attorneys (619-237-3490) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorneys, James Murdica and Sarah E. Johnston of Barnes & Thornburg LLP (310-284-3798) appear as counsels for defendant/moving party, McKesson Corporation

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Mckesson Corporation's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings, As To The "Federal Preemption" Issue is DENIED. At the hearing on July 29, 2016 all portions of the motion were denied except the portion seeking judgment based on federal preemption. The court now rejects the federal preemption argument as well. A motion for judgment on the pleadings may be granted only if the facts alleged in the challenged pleading or subject to judicial notice show that the pleading fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (County of Orange v. Association of Orange County Deputy Sheriffs (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 21, 32.) McKesson's reliance on answers filed by defendants is thus impermissible. The general rule under California law is that distributors of defective products are strictly liable. Plaintiff's factual allegations sufficiently invoke that general rule and thus support the causes of action asserted against McKesson. (Bostick v. Flex Equipment Co. Inc. (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 80, 88; complaint. 22, 24, 32, 33, 43- 45, 66, 76-81, 84, 85, 88, 89, 91-95, 97-99, 101, 103, 104, 107-109, 111, 112, 115, 117, 118, 120, 123, 125-129, 130-134, 136, 138, 140, 141.) McKesson's preemption argument that the reasoning in the decisions in Mensing and Bartlett applies to this case has never been accepted by California and is counter to the general rule. McKesson's argument in reliance on Bay Summit Community Assn. v. Shell Oil Co. (1996) 51 Cal.App.4th 762 is also misplaced because McKesson is alleged to be a distributor within the vertical chain of distribution. Nor is there any California authority supporting McKesson's federal preemption argument and it is implicitly rejected by the longstanding California case law on strict liability of distributors of products, including the seminal case of Vandermark. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order tracking the Court's ruling, verbatim and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendant Mckesson Corporation'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings, As To The "Federal Preemption" Issue

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jul-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kristen Barton of Gomez Trial Attorneys (619-237-3490) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, James Zachman

Attorney, Ahmed S. Diab of Gomez Trial Attorneys (619-237-3490) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, James Zachman

Attorneys, James Murdica and Sarah Johnston of Barnes & Thornburg (310-284-3798) appear as counsels for defendant/moving party McKesson Corporation, et al.

The Motion is argued by both sides. The Court rules in part and continues in part the motion. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

Defendant McKesson Corporation's motion for judgment on the pleadings is DENIED as to all issues except the issue of "Federal Preemption." A motion for judgment on the pleadings may be granted only if the facts alleged in the challenged pleading or subject to judicial notice show that the pleading fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (County of Orange v. Association of Orange County Deputy Sheriffs (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 21, 32.) McKesson's reliance on answers filed by defendants is thus impermissible. The general rule under California law is that distributors of defective products are strictly liable. Plaintiff's factual allegations sufficiently invoke that general rule and thus support the causes of action asserted against McKesson. (Bostick v. Flex Equipment Co. Inc. (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 80, 88; complaint. 22, 24, 32, 33, 43- 45, 66, 76-81, 84, 85, 88, 89, 91-95, 97-99, 101, 103, 104, 107-109, 111, 112, 115, 117, 118, 120, 123, 125-129, 130-134, 136, 138, 140, 141.) McKesson's argument in reliance on Bay Summit Community Assn. v. Shell Oil Co. (1996) 51 Cal.App.4th 762 is misplaced because McKesson is alleged to be a distributor within the vertical chain of distribution. Defendant's motion for judgment ON THE PLEADINGS, as to the "Federal Preemption" issue, is continued to September 8, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Dept. 302. Supplemental moving papers are due by 8/12/16; Supplemental opposition by 8/26/216; Five-page limit.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)
